# @autom8y/contente-tokens

> The single **owned source of truth** for the **Contente client-facing brand** —
> navy `#0e1b39` / blue `#2f6bff` / cream `#f8f5ef`; Spectral display, Plus Jakarta
> Sans UI, JetBrains Mono technical.

This package feeds the offline onboarding decks today and a future Path-C client
app tomorrow — from **one** owned source, so the two never drift into "two
Contente blues."

## What's in the box

| Surface | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `src/css/{colors,typography,fonts,spacing,elevation,motion}.css` | the 6 brand token sheets — **canonical**, byte-identical to the deck build's input |
| `src/css/styles.css` | the global entry aggregator (`@import`s the 6 sheets) |
| `tokens.json` | a **generated** DTCG projection of the brand, in Contente's own key-set — the structured surface for apps |
| `tokens.schema.json` | the Contente key-set contract (KEYS frozen, VALUES free) that `tokens.json` validates against |
| `index.mjs` | barrel: `tokens`, `cssDir`, `cssPath(name)` |

## The one rule: CSS is canonical, JSON is generated

The CSS sheets are the **upstream, authored** representation of every brand
value. `tokens.json` is a **one-way projection** produced by `scripts/generate.mjs`
— never hand-edit it. There is exactly one authored copy of each value (the CSS
`--custom-property` declaration); the DTCG view is derived. This precludes the
dual-authoring drift class entirely.

```
src/css/*.css   ──parse──▶   scripts/generate.mjs   ──emit──▶   tokens.json
 (CANONICAL)                  (deterministic)                    (GENERATED)
```

### Regenerate / guard

```sh
npm run generate   # parse the CSS → write tokens.json (deterministic)
npm run check      # regenerate in-memory and FAIL if the committed tokens.json drifted
```

`npm run check` is the CI guard: if a contributor edits a CSS value without
regenerating, or hand-edits `tokens.json`, `check` exits non-zero with a located
diff. Re-run `generate`, commit, done.

## Consuming the brand

**CSS consumers** (the offline deck build) resolve sheets through the package
`exports` map — no copy, no drift:

```js
import.meta.resolve('@autom8y/contente-tokens/css/colors');   // → file URL of colors.css
// or, from the barrel:
import { cssDir, cssPath } from '@autom8y/contente-tokens';
cssPath('colors.css');                                        // absolute path
```

**App consumers** (Path-C) import the structured DTCG tokens:

```js
import { tokens } from '@autom8y/contente-tokens';
tokens.color.blue['600'].$value;   // "#2f6bff"
tokens.font.display.$value;        // "\"Spectral\", Georgia, …, serif"
tokens.space['4'].$value;          // "16px"
```

## DTCG shape

`tokens.json` follows the [DTCG-draft](https://tr.designtokens.org/) leaf shape —
`{ "$type": …, "$value": … }`, nested object groups, `additionalProperties:false`
discipline — the **same shape** the internal `autom8y-fe-skeleton` fleet uses.

It does **NOT** reuse the fe-skeleton **key-set**. That schema (`color.brand.*`,
`primary`, `slate`, Inter/indigo) is a **different, internal admin brand**. This
package carries Contente's **own** key-set (`color.navy.900`, `color.blue.600`,
`font.display`, …) and shares **zero** keys with it. Same shape, different brand.

The projection is **lossless**: every one of the 122 brand custom properties
becomes a DTCG leaf — colors, font families, weights, sizes, spacing, radii,
shadows, easings, durations — so a Path-C app inherits the **entire** brand with
no hand-authoring.

## Versioning

- **PATCH** — docs / generator-internal change (no token change).
- **MINOR** — a CSS **value** change the consumers re-pull (additive / non-breaking).
- **MAJOR** — a **key-set** change (rename/remove a property, or a DTCG structural
  change) — it can break a consumer's key lookups.

## Provenance

The brand originates in the Contente design system
(`contente-design-system/project/tokens/`). This package is the owned propagation
point; the in-tree sheets are retired in favour of it (the deck build resolves
the package CSS directly).
